species: Vha68-3, a testis-specific subunit of the V-type adenosine triphosphate (ATP) synthase, predominantly localized in the tails of elongated spermatids, as a key age-related regulator of male fertility and spermatid elongation in Drosophila testes.
tissue: Vha68-3, a testis-specific subunit of the V-type adenosine triphosphate (ATP) synthase, predominantly localized in the tails of elongated spermatids...
cell_type: localized in the tails of elongated spermatids
sample_source: Fertility assessments revealed an age-dependent decline in male fertility in Vha68-3 KO flies.
age: by 40 days of age, KO flies exhibited a significant reduction in fertility compared with controls.
